title: Sorting Three Groups
content:
You are given an integer array nums. Each element in nums is 1, 2 or 3. In each operation, you can remove an element from nums. Return the minimum number of operations to make nums non-decreasing.

Example 1:

Input: nums = [2,1,3,2,1]

Output: 3

Explanation: One of the optimal solutions is to remove nums[0], nums[2] and nums[3].

Example 2:

Input: nums = [1,3,2,1,3,3]

Output: 2

Explanation: One of the optimal solutions is to remove nums[1] and nums[2].

Example 3:

Input: nums = [2,2,2,2,3,3]

Output: 0

Explanation: nums is already non-decreasing.

Constraints:

• 1 <= nums.length <= 100

• 1 <= nums[i] <= 3

Follow-up: Can you come up with an algorithm that runs in O(n) time complexity?
hints: The problem asks to change the array nums to make it sorted (i.e., all the 1s are on the left of 2s, and all the 2s are on the left of 3s.).

We can try all the possibilities to make nums indices range in [0, i) to 0 and [i, j) to 1 and [j, n) to 2. Note the ranges are left-close and right-open; each might be empty. Namely, 0 <= i <= j <= n.
Count the changes we need for each possibility by comparing the expected and original values at each index position.

solutions.explanation:
The minimum number of removals equals n minus the length of the longest non-decreasing subsequence (LNS). Since values are only 1, 2, 3, we keep a simple DP array of size 3 where dp[v] = length of the longest non-decreasing subsequence ending with value v+1.

For each element x, its best subsequence length is 1 plus the best among all dp values for values <= x. We update dp[x-1] with this value.

The answer is n - max(dp).

Time complexity: O(n). Space complexity: O(1).
